all.title: Legislators and Senior Officials
all.sub_division: Chief Executives, Senior Officials and Legislators
all.exposure_rationale: While aspects of policy analysis, data synthesis, and communication for legislators and senior officials could be augmented by AI, the core functions involve complex human judgment, negotiation, public representation, and ethical decision-making in unpredictable social and political environments. AI can assist, but cannot replace, the human element crucial to these roles, especially in the Indian context where personal relationships and nuanced understanding of local dynamics are paramount.

all.title: Managing Directors and Chief Executives
all.sub_division: Chief Executives, Senior Officials and Legislators
all.exposure_rationale: While managing directors and chief executives perform significant knowledge work, their roles heavily involve strategic decision-making, complex human interaction, leadership, and navigating unpredictable business environments. AI can assist with data analysis, reporting, and some strategic planning, but the core functions of vision setting, motivating teams, and high-stakes negotiation remain deeply human. The large employment size in India also suggests a significant informal sector component where AI adoption might be slower.

all.title: Business Services and Administration Managers
all.exposure_rationale: Business Services and Administration Managers primarily deal with planning, organizing, and overseeing administrative functions, which involves significant digital communication, data analysis, and strategic decision-making. AI can heavily assist with tasks like scheduling, resource allocation, performance monitoring, and report generation, making individual managers significantly more productive and potentially reducing the overall need for human oversight in routine administrative tasks. While human judgment and interpersonal skills remain crucial, the core work product is largely digital and information-based.

all.title: Sales, Marketing and Development Managers
all.exposure_rationale: This role involves significant strategic planning, market analysis, and communication, which are increasingly supported and enhanced by AI tools. While human relationship building and negotiation remain crucial, AI can automate market research, personalize marketing campaigns, and optimize sales processes, making individual managers significantly more productive and potentially reducing the overall need for human intervention in routine tasks.

all.title: Production Managers in Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries
all.exposure_rationale: While production managers in agriculture, forestry, and fisheries involve significant on-the-ground physical presence and decision-making in unpredictable environments, AI can increasingly assist with data analysis for crop/livestock management, resource optimization, and predictive analytics. The core management function still requires human judgment and interaction in the physical world, but AI can automate and optimize many information-processing and planning tasks.

all.title: Manufacturing, Mining, Construction and Distribution Managers
all.exposure_rationale: This role involves significant knowledge work, including planning, strategizing, and decision-making, which AI can heavily support through data analysis, predictive modeling, and optimization. While there's a physical component in overseeing operations, much of the core managerial function is digital and information-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and potential restructuring.

all.title: Professional Services Managers
all.exposure_rationale: Professional Services Managers primarily deal with planning, organizing, and overseeing service delivery, which involves significant digital communication, data analysis, and strategic decision-making. salaried.exposure_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and requires real-time human presence in unpredictable outdoor environments to sell goods. While AI might marginally influence supply chains or digital payments, the core tasks of physical inventory management and face-to-face street-level negotiation cannot be automated by current AI technologies.

salaried.title: Refuse Workers
salaried.sub_division: Refuse Workers and Other Elementary Workers
salaried.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves strenuous physical labor and manual handling of waste in unpredictable outdoor environments, which are tasks currently beyond the reach of AI. While large-scale waste management logistics may be optimized by software, the core work of refuse collection in the Indian context remains highly manual and physically demanding.

salaried.title: Other Elementary Workers
salaried.sub_division: Refuse Workers and Other Elementary Workers
salaried.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves physical, manual labor in unpredictable environments, such as refuse collection and miscellaneous street-level tasks, which cannot be digitized. Given the low wages and the informal nature of this work in India, there is no economic or technical incentive for AI or robotics to replace these workers in the foreseeable future.

self_employed.title: Managing Directors and Chief Executives
self_employed.sub_division: Chief Executives, Senior Officials and Legislators
self_employed.exposure_rationale: While managing directors and chief executives perform significant knowledge work, their roles heavily involve strategic decision-making, complex human interaction, leadership, and navigating unpredictable business environments. AI can assist with data analysis, reporting, and some strategic planning, but the core functions of vision setting, motivating teams, and high-stakes negotiation remain deeply human. The large employment size in India also suggests a significant informal sector component where AI adoption might be slower.

self_employed.title: Business Services and Administration Managers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Business Services and Administration Managers primarily deal with planning, organizing, and overseeing administrative functions, which involves significant digital communication, data analysis, and strategic decision-making. AI can heavily assist with tasks like scheduling, resource allocation, performance monitoring, and report generation, making individual managers significantly more productive and potentially reducing the overall need for human oversight in routine administrative tasks. While human judgment and interpersonal skills remain crucial, the core work product is largely digital and information-based.

self_employed.title: Sales, Marketing and Development Managers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This role involves significant strategic planning, market analysis, and communication, which are increasingly supported and enhanced by AI tools. While human relationship building and negotiation remain crucial, AI can automate market research, personalize marketing campaigns, and optimize sales processes, making individual managers significantly more productive and potentially reducing the overall need for human intervention in routine tasks.

self_employed.title: Production Managers in Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries
self_employed.exposure_rationale: While production managers in agriculture, forestry, and fisheries involve significant on-the-ground physical presence and decision-making in unpredictable environments, AI can increasingly assist with data analysis for crop/livestock management, resource optimization, and predictive analytics. The core management function still requires human judgment and interaction in the physical world, but AI can automate and optimize many information-processing and planning tasks.

self_employed.title: Manufacturing, Mining, Construction and Distribution Managers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This role involves significant knowledge work, including planning, strategizing, and decision-making, which AI can heavily support through data analysis, predictive modeling, and optimization. While there's a physical component in overseeing operations, much of the core managerial function is digital and information-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and potential restructuring.

self_employed.title: Information and Communications Technology Services Manager
self_employed.exposure_rationale: As an ICT Services Manager, the role involves significant digital interaction, strategic planning, and oversight of technology services. While human judgment and team management remain crucial, AI can increasingly assist with data analysis, resource optimization, project management, and even some decision-making processes, making managers more productive and potentially reducing the number needed for certain tasks.

self_employed.title: Professional Services Managers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Professional Services Managers primarily deal with planning, organizing, and overseeing service delivery, which involves significant digital communication, data analysis, and strategic decision-making. While human interaction and judgment remain crucial, AI can increasingly assist with project management, resource allocation, performance analysis, and even client communication, making workers significantly more productive and potentially reducing the number of managers needed for routine oversight.

self_employed.title: Hotel and Restaurant Managers
self_employed.sub_division: Hospitality, Retail and Other Services Managers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Hotel and Restaurant Managers involve a significant amount of human interaction, on-site problem-solving, and managing physical operations, which are difficult for AI to fully automate. While AI can assist with tasks like booking systems, inventory management, and customer service chatbots, the core functions of leadership, guest relations, and managing staff in a dynamic environment still require human presence and judgment.

self_employed.title: Retail and Wholesale Trade Managers
self_employed.sub_division: Hospitality, Retail and Other Services Managers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: While retail and wholesale trade managers involve significant human interaction and on-site presence, a substantial portion of their work involves data analysis, inventory management, sales forecasting, and strategic planning. AI can significantly automate and optimize these digital tasks, making managers more productive and potentially reducing the number of managers needed for certain functions. The Indian context of a large informal retail sector might temper the immediate impact, but formal retail is growing rapidly.

self_employed.title: Other Services Managers
self_employed.sub_division: Hospitality, Retail and Other Services Managers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Other Services Managers involve a significant amount of planning, organizing, coordinating, and decision-making, which are knowledge-based tasks where AI can provide substantial assistance. While human interaction and judgment remain crucial for managing teams and client relationships, AI can automate administrative tasks, analyze performance data, and optimize resource allocation, increasing productivity and potentially reducing the number of managers needed.

self_employed.title: Physical and Earth Science Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Physical and Earth Science Professionals primarily engage in knowledge work, analysis, and research, much of which is digital (data analysis, modeling, report writing). While some aspects require fieldwork or lab work, AI can significantly enhance productivity in data processing, simulation, and predictive modeling, making these professionals highly exposed to AI-driven changes.

self_employed.title: Life Sciences Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Life Sciences Professionals heavily rely on data analysis, research, and report writing, which are digital tasks highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While some aspects involve lab work or fieldwork, a significant portion of their core work product is digital, making them highly exposed to AI's transformative potential in research and analysis.

self_employed.title: Engineering Professionals (Excluding Electrotechnology)
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Engineering professionals, while often dealing with physical systems, rely heavily on digital tools for design, simulation, analysis, and project management. AI can significantly enhance productivity in these areas, automating routine design tasks, optimizing processes, and assisting with complex problem-solving, leading to high exposure.

self_employed.title: Electrotechnology Engineers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Electrotechnology Engineers primarily engage in design, analysis, and problem-solving, which are increasingly supported by AI tools for simulation, optimization, and automation of routine design tasks. While some aspects involve physical implementation and project management, a significant portion of their work is digital and knowledge-based, making them highly susceptible to AI augmentation and efficiency gains.

self_employed.title: Architects, Planners, Surveyors and Designers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves significant digital work like design, planning, and analysis using specialized software. While creativity and client interaction remain human-centric, AI can heavily assist in generating designs, optimizing plans, and analyzing data, making individual professionals much more productive and potentially reducing the overall demand for human labor in routine design tasks.

self_employed.title: Medical Doctors
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Medical doctors require extensive human interaction, empathy, physical examination, and complex decision-making in unpredictable situations, which are difficult for AI to fully replicate. While AI can assist with diagnostics, research, and administrative tasks, the core patient care and surgical procedures remain highly human-centric. The Indian context also implies varying levels of technology adoption across different healthcare settings.

self_employed.title: Nursing and Midwifery Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Nursing and midwifery professionals require significant in-person interaction, physical care, and real-time judgment in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with diagnostics, record-keeping, or administrative tasks, the core of the job involves direct patient care and human empathy, which are difficult to automate.

self_employed.title: Traditional and Complementary Medicine Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: While traditional and complementary medicine involves significant human interaction and physical examination, aspects like diagnosis, treatment planning, and research into herbal remedies or therapeutic approaches could be augmented by AI. However, the core of the profession relies heavily on personalized patient interaction, touch, and intuitive understanding, which are difficult for AI to replicate.

self_employed.title: Paramedical Practitioners
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Paramedical practitioners perform a mix of physical clinical tasks and diagnostic analysis that requires real-time human presence and manual dexterity. While AI can significantly assist in diagnostic interpretation and patient monitoring, the core of the job involves physical intervention, emergency response, and direct patient care that cannot be digitized.

self_employed.title: Veterinarians
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Veterinary work requires significant physical presence for examinations, surgeries, and manual procedures that AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist in diagnostic imaging, pathology analysis, and treatment planning, the core of the job involves handling live animals and real-time clinical judgment in unpredictable environments.

self_employed.title: Other Health Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This group includes diverse roles like physiotherapists, speech therapists, and dieticians, which combine digital diagnostic/planning tasks with essential physical therapy and interpersonal interaction. While AI can significantly enhance treatment planning, medical imaging analysis, and patient monitoring, the core requirement for hands-on physical intervention and real-time human empathy in the Indian healthcare context limits full automation.

self_employed.title: University and Higher Education Teachers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This role involves high-level knowledge work, research, and content creation, all of which are being significantly transformed by Generative AI. While the physical classroom presence and mentorship aspects provide a buffer, AI's ability to automate grading, lesson planning, and literature reviews increases productivity and reshapes the core pedagogical duties.

self_employed.title: Vocational Education Teachers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Vocational education involves a mix of theoretical knowledge and practical, hands-on training, which provides some protection against full automation. However, AI can significantly automate curriculum design, personalized student assessment, and the digital components of technical training, leading to high exposure for the knowledge-transfer aspects of the role.

self_employed.title: Secondary Education Teachers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Secondary education involves significant digital-adjacent tasks like lesson planning, grading, and content creation which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While the physical presence and interpersonal mentorship required in Indian classrooms provide a buffer, AI-driven personalized learning and administrative tools will substantially restructure how these professionals manage their workload.

self_employed.title: Primary School and Early Childhood Teachers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: While AI can significantly automate lesson planning, grading, and personalized content creation, the core of primary and early childhood education relies on physical supervision, emotional intelligence, and social development. In the Indian context, the high demand for human-led classroom management and the physical nature of childcare provide a substantial barrier to full automation.

self_employed.title: Other Teaching Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This category includes diverse roles like private tutors and special education teachers whose work involves significant information delivery and curriculum design, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the low median pay suggests a large informal sector with less immediate tech integration, the core tasks of lesson planning and content creation are increasingly being automated by generative AI tools.

self_employed.title: Finance Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves high-level digital tasks such as financial analysis, risk assessment, and reporting, which are increasingly being automated by sophisticated AI algorithms and large language models. While high-level strategic advisory and Indian regulatory compliance require human judgment, the core work product is digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to significant productivity shifts and workforce restructuring.

self_employed.title: Administration Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves predominantly digital tasks such as policy analysis, organizational planning, and administrative coordination, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and augmentation. While some high-level strategic judgment and stakeholder management are required, the core work product is information-based and can be significantly streamlined by generative AI and data analytics tools.

self_employed.title: Sales, Marketing and Public Relations Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and information-based, involving content creation, market analysis, and strategic communication which are core strengths of Generative AI. While high-level relationship management and local cultural nuances in the Indian market provide some protection, the majority of tasks like copywriting, lead generation, and campaign optimization are facing significant restructuring and automation.

self_employed.title: Software and Application Developers, and Analysts
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, with core tasks like coding, debugging, and system analysis being directly impacted by Large Language Models and automated development tools. While high-level architecture and complex problem-solving still require human oversight, AI significantly increases individual productivity, potentially reducing the total number of workers needed for routine development tasks.

self_employed.title: Database and Network Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital and centered on data management, network optimization, and security, all of which are domains where AI excels at pattern recognition and automation. AI tools are rapidly automating routine tasks like database tuning, anomaly detection, and network configuration, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of workers required for standard maintenance.

self_employed.title: Legal Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Legal work in India involves heavy document analysis, research, and drafting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and efficiency gains. While courtroom advocacy and client negotiations require human judgment and physical presence, the high volume of digital information processing makes the profession highly exposed to restructuring.

self_employed.title: Librarians, Archivists and Curators
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves high levels of information organization, cataloging, and research, which are tasks increasingly streamlined by AI and automated digital systems. While curators and archivists require human judgment for physical preservation and cultural context, the core digital workflows of data management and information retrieval are highly exposed to AI-driven efficiency gains.

self_employed.title: Social and Religious Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: While these roles involve information processing and counseling that AI can assist with, the core of the work is deeply rooted in human empathy, spiritual guidance, and community leadership within the Indian social context. The low median pay and high reliance on face-to-face interpersonal trust create a significant barrier to full AI automation, though AI may assist in administrative or research tasks.

self_employed.title: Authors, Journalists and Linguists
self_employed.exposure_rationale: The work product of authors, journalists, and linguists is entirely digital, consisting of text and language processing which aligns perfectly with the core capabilities of Large Language Models. While high-level investigative journalism and creative literature still require human nuance, the vast majority of routine reporting, content creation, and translation tasks are facing immediate and massive restructuring due to AI automation.

self_employed.title: Creative and Performing Artists
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves high levels of digital content creation (writing, music, visual arts) where Generative AI is rapidly automating core tasks and enhancing productivity. While live performance and cultural nuance provide a buffer in the Indian context, the shift toward AI-assisted digital media significantly reshapes the professional landscape and competitive requirements.

self_employed.title: Physical and Engineering Science Technicians
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a mix of digital data analysis and physical laboratory or field work, such as testing equipment and monitoring industrial processes. While AI can significantly automate data interpretation, simulation, and reporting tasks, the requirement for physical presence to maintain hardware and conduct manual experiments provides a buffer against full automation.

self_employed.title: Mining, Manufacturing and Construction Supervisors
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of physical oversight, safety inspections, and real-time coordination of manual labor on-site, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI is increasingly used for predictive maintenance, resource scheduling, and automated progress monitoring, which will streamline the administrative and planning components of the job.

self_employed.title: Process Control Technicians
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Process control technicians operate in a hybrid environment where they monitor digital systems to manage physical industrial processes. While AI can significantly automate data monitoring, anomaly detection, and optimization of control loops, the role still requires physical presence for equipment maintenance, manual overrides, and on-site troubleshooting in Indian industrial settings.

self_employed.title: Life Science Technicians and Related Associate Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a significant mix of physical laboratory work, such as preparing specimens and maintaining equipment, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, the data analysis, documentation, and reporting aspects of the role are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automated processing, leading to moderate overall exposure.

self_employed.title: Medical and Pharmaceutical Technicians
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This role involves a significant mix of physical laboratory work, such as handling biological samples and operating diagnostic machinery, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, the information-processing component—analyzing test results, identifying patterns in imaging, and data recording—is highly susceptible to AI-driven diagnostic tools that can increase efficiency and accuracy.

self_employed.title: Nursing and Midwifery Associate Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with patient monitoring, documentation, and diagnostic support, the core of this role involves physical bedside care, manual procedures, and emotional support that require a human presence. In the Indian context, the high volume of hands-on clinical work and the reliance on interpersonal interaction in healthcare settings provide a significant buffer against automation.

self_employed.title: Traditional and Complementary Medicine Associate Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical interaction, manual therapy, and patient assessment that cannot be digitized. However, AI exposure is moderate because diagnostic support, herbal formulation databases, and treatment planning in traditional medicine are increasingly being augmented by digital tools and information processing.

self_employed.title: Veterinary Technicians and Assistants
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation requires significant physical presence for animal handling, clinical procedures, and laboratory work that cannot be digitized. While AI may assist in diagnostic analysis or administrative record-keeping, the core tasks involve manual dexterity and real-time interaction with animals in a physical environment.

self_employed.title: Other Health Associate Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a mix of technical medical tasks and direct patient interaction, which provides a buffer against full automation. While AI can significantly assist in diagnostic support, record-keeping, and monitoring, the physical nature of healthcare delivery and the need for human empathy in the Indian clinical context keep exposure at a moderate level.

self_employed.title: Financial and Mathematical Associate Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves digital tasks such as financial calculations, data processing, and statistical analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While some human oversight is required for Indian regulatory compliance and client communication, the core work product is digital and can be significantly streamlined or replaced by advanced algorithms and generative AI.

self_employed.title: Sales and Purchasing Agents and Brokers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves significant digital communication, data analysis, and negotiation, all of which are increasingly being augmented or automated by AI-driven CRM and procurement tools. While high-level relationship management and physical inspections provide some protection, the core tasks of matching buyers with sellers and processing transactions are highly susceptible to AI efficiency gains.

self_employed.title: Business Service Agents
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves digital-centric tasks such as brokering, clearing, and coordinating business services, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and algorithmic matching. While some human negotiation and relationship management are required, the core functions of information processing and transaction facilitation are increasingly being handled by AI platforms, significantly reducing the need for human intermediaries.

self_employed.title: Administrative and Specialized Secretaries
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves high-volume digital tasks such as scheduling, document preparation, and information management, all of which are core strengths of Large Language Models and AI agents. In the Indian corporate context, AI can automate the vast majority of these administrative workflows, significantly reducing the need for human intermediaries for routine coordination and specialized documentation.

self_employed.title: Legal, Social and Religious Associate Professionals

self_employed.exposure_rationale: This group involves significant information processing, documentation, and research tasks that are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and augmentation. While social and religious roles require a degree of human empathy and physical presence, the legal and administrative components are increasingly being reshaped by AI tools for drafting and case analysis.

self_employed.title: Sports and Fitness Workers

self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation requires physical presence, manual demonstration of techniques, and real-time human interaction in a physical environment. self_employed.title: Administrative Associate Professionals
self_employed.sub_division: Legal, Social, Cultural and Related Associate Professionals
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves high volumes of routine digital tasks such as scheduling, document preparation, and information management, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While some human coordination is required, the core work product is digital and can be significantly streamlined or replaced by AI agents and large language models.

self_employed.title: Information and Communication Technology Operations and User Support Technicians
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation is highly digital, involving troubleshooting, system monitoring, and user support that can increasingly be handled by AI-driven diagnostic tools and automated help desks. While some hardware-related tasks require physical presence, the majority of the work involves software interfaces and remote support, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and task automation.

self_employed.title: Telecommunication and Broadcasting Technicians
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This role involves a significant physical component, such as installing, maintaining, and repairing hardware and infrastructure in the field, which provides a buffer against AI. However, AI is increasingly automating the diagnostic, monitoring, and signal optimization aspects of the job, reducing the time spent on troubleshooting and potentially lowering the demand for human technicians.

self_employed.title: General Office Clerks
self_employed.exposure_rationale: General office clerks perform routine digital tasks such as data entry, document filing, and basic correspondence, which are highly susceptible to automation through LLMs and RPA. In the Indian context, as government and private sectors digitize, these roles face significant restructuring as AI can process information and manage workflows more efficiently than human staff.

self_employed.title: Secretaries (General)
self_employed.exposure_rationale: General secretaries perform tasks that are almost entirely digital and information-based, such as scheduling, drafting correspondence, and organizing data, all of which are core strengths of Large Language Models. In the Indian context, while some interpersonal coordination remains, the rapid adoption of AI assistants significantly reduces the need for human intervention in routine clerical and administrative workflows.

self_employed.title: Keyboard Operators
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves routine, digital information processing and data entry that is highly susceptible to automation through Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and Large Language Models. Since the work product is entirely digital and requires minimal complex judgment or physical presence, AI can already perform these tasks with higher speed and accuracy than human operators.

self_employed.title: Tellers, Money Collectors and Related Clerks
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves routine information processing, financial transactions, and record-keeping that are almost entirely digital or digitizable. In the Indian context, the rapid expansion of UPI, digital banking, and AI-driven kiosks is aggressively automating these roles, leaving very little that requires human intervention beyond occasional troubleshooting.

self_employed.title: Client Information Workers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves routine information processing, answering queries, and managing digital records, all of which are primary targets for Large Language Models and automated chatbots. Since the work product is almost entirely digital and communication-based, AI can automate the vast majority of core tasks, leading to significant workforce restructuring in the Indian service sector.

self_employed.title: Numerical Clerks
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Numerical clerks perform routine digital tasks such as data entry, basic bookkeeping, and payroll processing, which are highly susceptible to automation through AI and RPA. Since the work product is entirely digital and follows structured rules, AI can perform these tasks with higher speed and accuracy, leading to significant workforce restructuring in the Indian formal sector.

self_employed.title: Material Recording and Transport Clerks
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves routine information processing, inventory tracking, and documentation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and digital integration. While some physical presence in warehouses is required, the core clerical tasks are increasingly handled by automated systems and AI-powered logistics software, significantly reducing the need for manual record-keeping.

self_employed.title: Other Clerical Support Workers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves routine information processing, filing, and administrative tasks that are fundamentally digital or paper-based and easily digitized. Given the low complexity and high predictability of these clerical duties, AI and automation can already perform the majority of the core workload, leading to significant displacement risk in the Indian formal sector.

self_employed.title: Travel Attendants, Conductors and Guides
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical presence, real-time human interaction, and safety responsibilities that AI cannot replicate. self_employed.title: Cooks
self_employed.exposure_rationale: Cooking is a highly physical and sensory occupation requiring manual dexterity, real-time adjustments to ingredients, and physical presence in a kitchen environment. While AI might assist with recipe generation or inventory management, the core tasks of food preparation and execution remain shielded from automation due to the high cost and complexity of robotics compared to human labor in the Indian context.

self_employed.title: Waiters and Bartenders
self_employed.exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks like serving food and drinks and real-time human interaction in a physical environment, which are difficult to automate. self_employed.title: Hairdressers, Beauticians and Related Workers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation requires high-dexterity manual labor and physical presence to perform services directly on the human body, which AI cannot replicate. self_employed.title: Building and Housekeeping Supervisors
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This role requires a physical presence to inspect premises, manage cleaning staff, and handle on-site maintenance issues, which limits AI's direct impact. While AI can assist with scheduling, inventory tracking, and reporting, the core responsibilities involve real-world observation and human management in a physical environment.

self_employed.title: Other Personal Services Workers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This category includes roles like astrologers, undertakers, and pet groomers, which require physical presence and real-time human interaction. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like scheduling or generating horoscopes, the core service remains deeply rooted in the physical world and personal emotional connection.

self_employed.title: Street and Market Salespersons
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally rooted in the physical world, requiring real-time human interaction, physical presence, and the manual handling of goods in unpredictable outdoor environments. self_employed.title: Shop Salespersons
self_employed.exposure_rationale: The core of this role involves physical presence, inventory management, and face-to-face customer interaction within a physical retail environment, which provides a natural barrier to AI. self_employed.title: Cashiers and Ticket Clerks
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation involves routine transactional tasks and information processing that are highly susceptible to automation through AI-driven self-checkout systems and digital payment interfaces. While the Indian context still relies on cash and human interaction in many retail segments, the rapid adoption of UPI and automated ticketing systems in transport and cinema is significantly reducing the need for human clerks.

self_employed.title: Other Sales Workers
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This category involves a mix of physical retail presence and interpersonal interaction, which provides a buffer against full automation. self_employed.title: Child Care Workers and Teachers' Aides
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation requires high levels of physical presence, emotional intelligence, and real-time supervision of children in unpredictable environments. self_employed.title: Personal Care Workers in Health Services
self_employed.exposure_rationale: This occupation requires significant physical presence and manual dexterity for tasks like bathing, feeding, and mobility assistance, which AI cannot perform. self_employed.title: Protective Service Workers

self_employed.title: Animal Producers

self_employed.title: Mixed Crop and Animal Workers
